title: Common Preprocessing Mistakes
concept: NLP pipelines are highly sensitive to data cleaning choices; aggressive stripping can delete critical features (like punctuation in sentiment).

exercises.prompt: What does 'preprocessing' mean in the context of NLP?

options.text: Cleaning and preparing raw text for a model
Writing code to build an entirely new neural network

exercises.explain: Preprocessing is the step where we clean and format raw text so an AI model can process it effectively.

cards.title: Can Cleaning Hurt Your AI?
cards.body: Have you ever sent a text message with five exclamation points to show how excited you are? In AI, those tiny punctuation marks are huge clues. If we clean our text too aggressively, we might accidentally make our AI deaf to excitement, anger, and sarcasm!

cards.title: What is Over-Cleaning?
cards.body: Text preprocessing is the way we clean and prepare text before giving it to an AI. However, over-cleaning—like blindly deleting all punctuation, emojis, or symbols—can accidentally strip away the exact clues (like '!!!' or '?') that the AI needs to understand meaning and sentiment.

cards.title: The Over-Cleaning Trap
cards.body: Imagine cleaning a messy bedroom. If you organize it, it is easy to find your books. But if you throw away every single loose piece of paper, you might accidentally throw away your homework! Similarly, aggressively cleaning your text might delete important punctuation clues that the AI needs to understand the mood.

cards.title: Sarcasm and Sentiment
cards.body: If a customer writes: "Oh great, another delay...", the ellipsis (...) and sarcasm convey frustration. If we aggressively clean it to just "oh great another delay", the AI might think the user is happy because of the word "great". Keeping punctuation helps preserve this critical context!

cards.title: Myth: Clean Text is Always Better
cards.body: People often think that the cleaner and simpler the text is, the smarter the AI will be. In reality, modern NLP models are smart enough to handle punctuation, and removing it often makes them perform worse on sentiment tasks.

cards.title: Visualizing Lost Data
cards.body: Let's look at how aggressive cleaning changes the data we feed into our AI. Notice how the feeling changes when the punctuation vanishes!

left.text: "I hate waiting!!!" (Shows strong frustration and anger)

exercises.prompt: Why does aggressive text preprocessing sometimes make NLP models perform worse?

options.text: It increases the file size of the database.
It deletes important context clues like question marks and exclamation points.
It makes the model train much slower.
It changes the language of the original text.

exercises.explain: Modern NLP models need context. Stripping everything leaves only raw words, removing the emotional signals that punctuation and capitalization provide.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Removing all punctuation is always the best first step in any NLP pipeline.
exercises.explain: Punctuation like exclamation marks and question marks are vital features for tasks like sentiment analysis.

exercises.prompt: Complete the sentence about NLP preprocessing.
exercises.sentence: Aggressively stripping all _____ can ruin a model's ability to detect sarcasm and excitement.
exercises.explain: Punctuation marks like exclamation points and question marks carry emotional weight that helps models classify sentiment.

exercises.prompt: Two AI models are built to detect angry customers. Which one has a better preprocessing strategy?

options.text: AI Model A: It deletes punctuation because symbols confuse all AI models.
AI Model B: It keeps exclamation points because they signal strong excitement or anger.
AI Model C: It replaces all words with numbers to save memory.

exercises.explain: AI Model B is correct because removing all punctuation destroys the sentiment signals (like the exclamation points or question marks) needed to analyze emotions.

exercises.prompt: Your team is building a tool to detect customer frustration. Why is it a bad idea to aggressively strip all punctuation from the text before sending it to your model?

options.text: It speeds up the pipeline by removing useless bytes.
It strips away crucial emotional signals like exclamation points.
It makes it easier for the model to detect subtle sarcasm.
It forces the model to only focus on nouns and verbs.

exercises.explain: Removing punctuation like exclamation points or question marks completely destroys context clues that sentiment analysis models rely on to detect strong emotions or confusion.
